content:
# Real-world benchmark: unified keyframe sampling — clip 0057 (2026-04-04)

**Date:** 2026-04-04
**Pipeline version:** unified keyframe sampling (fused RAUNE+depth pass-1, removes separate auto_calibrate() subprocess)

## Test clip

- File: `DJI_20251101112849_0057_D.MP4`
- Resolution: 3840×2160 (4K), HEVC, 119.88fps
- Duration: 41.3s → 4,943 frames

## Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total wall time | **12m 12.312s** |
| User time | 28m 29.563s |
| Sys time | 3m 36.355s |
| Output path | `working/graded/DJI_20251101112849_0057_D_graded_unified-keyframe_20260404.mp4` |

## Stage breakdown

| Stage | Time |
|-------|------|
| Pre-grading (calibration + pass-1 + batch depth) | ~4m 54s (03:38:18 → 03:43:12) |
| Grading pass (4943 frames) | ~7m 9s (03:43:22 → 03:50:31) |

## Comparison vs combined-LUT build (same clip, same day)

| Stage | Combined-LUT | Unified-Keyframe | Δ |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|---|
| Pre-grading | 6m 12s | 4m 54s | **−1m 18s (−21%)** |
| Grading pass | 7m 20s | 7m 9s | −11s (−2.5%) |
| **Total wall time** | **13m 42.9s** | **12m 12.3s** | **−1m 30.6s (−11%)** |

## Key finding

The unified keyframe PR saves ~1m 30s (11%) on a 41s 4K clip. The gain is entirely in pre-grading: fusing RAUNE+depth into a single pass-1 sweep eliminates one subprocess round-trip and the redundant frame decode previously used by auto_calibrate(). The grading pass throughput (~11.2 fps) is unchanged, confirming the hot path was unaffected.
